description:
Your software team just finished a small feature for the company website and you, the web-developer on release duty, must prepare a MINOR semantic version bump.  
    The project lives in  
        /home/user/projects/website  

    Current situation before you start (do **not** change these lines manually, just inspect them if you wish):

        1. /home/user/projects/website/package.json  
           - contains `"version": "1.2.3"` (exactly, with double quotes).  

        2. /home/user/projects/website/CHANGELOG.md  
           - its first line is `## [1.2.3] - 2024-05-20` followed by two more lines.

    Your release-manager checklist (perform **all** steps from the terminal):

        a. Bump the version field in /home/user/projects/website/package.json from 1.2.3 to 1.3.0 (a MINOR bump).  

        b. Prepend a brand-new section to /home/user/projects/website/CHANGELOG.md **exactly** as shown here, including blank lines and the markdown headings:

           ```
           ## [1.3.0] - 2024-06-01
           ### Added
           - Implemented blog post search feature.

           ```

           •  The new section must appear ABOVE every older section.  
           •  Leave the existing 1.2.3 section untouched and immediately below the new one.  

        c. Create a new release log file at  
              /home/user/projects/website/release.log  
           with **one single line** (no extra blank lines, include the trailing period in the message):

           ```
           2024-06-01 1.3.0 Minor release published
           ```

    The automated tests will check:

        1. /home/user/projects/website/package.json now contains `"version": "1.3.0"` and *no other part of the file changed*.  
        2. /home/user/projects/website/CHANGELOG.md begins with the four lines shown above and the old 1.2.3 entry still follows exactly as before.  
        3. /home/user/projects/website/release.log exists and contains precisely the single line specified.  

    You do NOT have to stage, commit, or push anything to git; only the filesystem changes matter.

    Hint: This can be completed with roughly 3–4 shell commands (open editor or use sed, echo, etc.).  
    When you have finished, listing the first 20 lines of both package.json and CHANGELOG.md plus the contents of release.log is a good way to visually verify your work.
truth:
Starting state that will exist before the agent begins:

    1. Directory: /home/user/projects/website (already created).

    2. File: /home/user/projects/website/package.json  
       Contents (including final newline):
{
  "name": "awesome-website",
  "version": "1.2.3",
  "description": "A demo website",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js"
  }
}

    3. File: /home/user/projects/website/CHANGELOG.md  
       Contents (including final newline):
## [1.2.3] - 2024-05-20
### Fixed
- Resolved navigation bug.

    Expected final state after successful task completion:

    1. /home/user/projects/website/package.json  
       must be identical to the starting file **except** for the version line, which must now read:
  "version": "1.3.0",

    2. /home/user/projects/website/CHANGELOG.md  
       must contain exactly (including blank line after bullet):

## [1.3.0] - 2024-06-01
### Added
- Implemented blog post search feature.

## [1.2.3] - 2024-05-20
### Fixed
- Resolved navigation bug.

    3. /home/user/projects/website/release.log  
       must exist with permissions 0644 and contain exactly (including newline at end):
2024-06-01 1.3.0 Minor release published
name: task_000000_7d66dd84
